heading: Advisory Board for Cuba Broadcasting
label: 1465c Advisory Board for Cuba Broadcasting
notes: Repeal of SectionSection repealed upon transmittal of determination by President under that democratically elected government in Cuba is in power, see .section 6063(c)(3) of this titlesection 6037(c) of this title

References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in subsec. (a), was in the original “this division”, and was translated as reading “this Act”, meaning , which enacted this subchapter, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 98–111The Television Broadcasting to Cuba Act, referred to in subsec. (b), is part D of title II of , , , which is classified principally to subchapter V–B (§ 1465aa et seq.) of this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 101–246104 Stat. 58section 1465aa of this titleFeb. 16, 1990

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Radio Broadcasting to Cuba Act which comprises this subchapter, and not as part of the United States Information and Educational Exchange Act of 1948 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Establishment; membership; chairperson
children.label: a Establishment; membership; chairperson
children.text: There is established within the Office of the President the Advisory Board for Cuba Broadcasting (in this subchapter referred to as the “Advisory Board”). The Advisory Board shall consist of nine members, appointed by the President by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, of whom not more than five shall be members of the same political party. The President shall designate one member of the Advisory Board to serve as chairperson.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Review; recommendations
children.label: b Review; recommendations
children.text: The Advisory Board shall review the effectiveness of the activities carried out under this subchapter and the Television Broadcasting to Cuba Act [ et seq.] and shall make recommendations to the President and the Broadcasting Advisory Board of Governors as it may consider necessary.22 U.S.C. 1465aa2So in original. The word “Advisory” probably should not appear.2

children.heading: Terms; vacancies
children.label: c Terms; vacancies
children.text: In appointing the initial voting members of the Advisory Board, the President shall designate three members to serve for a term of three years, three members to serve for a term of two years, and three members to serve for a term of one year. Thereafter, the term of each member of the Advisory Board shall be three years. The President shall appoint, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, members to fill vacancies occurring prior to the expiration of a term, in which case the members so appointed shall serve for the remainder of such term. Any member whose term has expired may serve until his successor has been appointed and qualified.

children.heading: Ex officio members
children.label: d Ex officio members
children.text: The head of the Cuba Service and the head of the Television Marti Service shall serve, ex officio, as members of the Advisory Board.

children.heading: Compensation; travel expenses
children.label: e Compensation; travel expenses
children.text: Members of the Advisory Board appointed by the President shall, while attending meetings of the Advisory Board or while engaged in duties relating to such meetings or in other activities of the Advisory Board pursuant to this section, including traveltime, be entitled to receive compensation equal to the daily equivalent of the compensation prescribed for level V of the Executive Schedule under . While away from their homes or regular places of business they may be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, as authorized by law () for persons in the Government service employed intermittently. The ex officio members of the Advisory Board shall not be entitled to any compensation under this section, but may be allowed travel expenses as provided in the preceding sentence.section 5316 of title 55 U.S.C. 5703

children.heading: Procurement powers of Advisory Board
children.label: f Procurement powers of Advisory Board
children.text: The Advisory Board may, to the extent it deems necessary to carry out its functions under this section, procure supplies, services, and other personal property, including specialized electronic equipment.

children.heading: Indefinite life span of Advisory Board
children.label: g Indefinite life span of Advisory Board
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Advisory Board shall remain in effect indefinitely.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $130,000 to carry out the provisions of this section.
